title: M.Sc Forensic Sciences with specialization in Questioned Documents and Fingerprints
content: The M.Sc Forensic Science program with a specialization in Questioned Documents and Fingerprints is an postgraduate course designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of forensic science principles, focusing on the analysis of questioned documents and fingerprint examination. Graduates are well-equipped to contribute to the criminal justice system and pursue diverse and rewarding careers in the forensic field. Graduates of the M.Sc. Forensic Science with specialization in Questioned Documents and Fingerprints can pursue various career paths, including: Forensic Document Examiner and Fingerprint Analyst. The curriculum is designed to balance theoretical knowledge with practical skills through a combination of lectures, laboratory work, internships, and field training. 01 Develop a strong theoretical and practical understanding of questioned document examination and fingerprint analysis, including handwriting, signature verification, and latent print identification. 02 Train students in advanced techniques for detecting document forgeries, alterations, counterfeit currencies, and ink or paper analysis using specialized instruments. 03 Equip students with skills in fingerprint classification and comparison, including ridge pattern analysis, AFIS (Automated Fingerprint Identification System), and chemical development methods. 04 Promote analytical thinking and research capabilities in areas such as biometric identification, signature dynamics, and emerging trends in document security features. 05 Prepare students for professional roles in forensic laboratories, law enforcement, banking fraud detection units, and judicial systems, ensuring adherence to legal and ethical standards in evidence handling. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 09 10 01 02 03 01 02
